mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.2 percent, labor market slack signals weakening consumer spending power and rising recession probability, causing equity valuations to contract over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 65 percent of periods where UNRATE exceeds 4.2 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansions in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business confidence, driving durable goods demand and corporate revenue growth that compounds into positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce statistically significant positive 60-day forward returns, or interaction term coefficient is insignificant at 90 percent confidence.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions, industrial production peaks signal capacity constraints and impending supply bottlenecks, triggering margin compression and negative short-term equity repricing within five trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO peaks during late-cycle phases do not precede negative 5-day S&P 500 returns, or the signal loses predictive power when GDP growth exceeds 3 percent annually.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 5.0 percent, restrictive monetary policy directly suppresses risk appetite and compresses equity multiples, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Periods with FEDFUNDS above 5.0 percent yield positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55 percent of cases, invalidating the restrictive policy penalty.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirm robust labor demand and wage growth, expanding household consumption capacity and driving sustained positive equity returns over three months.
failure_condition: The joint signal of PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline fails to predict positive 60-day returns, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product reflects stronger aggregate economic activity and corporate earnings momentum, which translates into immediate positive market repricing over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 levels show no predictive relationship with 5-day forward returns, or the signal generates negative alpha after transaction costs in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production crosses above 102.0, manufacturing capacity utilization improves and supply chain constraints ease, boosting corporate profit margins and generating positive equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: INDPRO readings above 102.0 do not precede positive 20-day S&P 500 returns, or the threshold effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shifts.
